description: Validate elastic stored-energy release into rigid-body launch motion using spring/compression labels, release events, trajectory response, and energy envelope checks. Spring launch is only the smoke family.
physical_assumptions: An elastic launcher stores energy through a structured spring constant and compression label.
The launched body starts still before the release event.
The release event is explicit and references launcher and target ids.
Post-release motion must show launch speed and displacement consistent with the stored energy envelope.
Kinetic energy must not exceed the declared stored-energy bound unless another force source is declared.
verifier_rules: Payload mass, spring constant, and compression are positive.
Launched body initial velocity is near zero.
Release event exists and references launcher/target ids.
Post-release speed exceeds the expected minimum.
Height gain and forward displacement exceed expected minima.
Post-release kinetic energy stays within the declared energy ratio.
repair_suggestions: Export a spring_events release event before checking trajectory response.
Remove hidden initial velocity from the launched body.
Use stored elastic energy to derive launch speed bounds instead of keyframing flight.
Add another force label if motion exceeds the elastic energy envelope.
